semantic.role: Infrastructure module providing a pluggable, path-derived credential store for securely managing LLM API keys and secrets.
responsibilities: Expose the CredentialStore class that wraps a CredentialBackend to get, set, and delete secrets.
Provide createProjectLLMCredentialStore to instantiate a store for a specific project directory, deriving a unique secret name via SHA-256 hashing.
Provide createGlobalLLMCredentialStore to instantiate a store for a global user directory with the same deterministic naming.
Define the CredentialSource type indicating whether the backend is available ('keychain') or not ('missing').
Fall back to createDefaultCredentialBackend when no explicit backend is provided.
outOfScope: Credential encryption at rest (delegated to the backend implementation).
Managing multiple credentials per store (only a single secret name per instance).
invariants: The CredentialStore always uses a backend conforming to CredentialBackend interface.
Secret names are deterministically derived from paths using SHA-256.
Factory functions always return a valid CredentialStore instance regardless of backend availability.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a stable, reusable credential management layer for the LLM infrastructure that decouples secret storage from specific platform backends (e.g., keychain).
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Recent refactoring to stabilize the credential store API and ensure deterministic secret naming across project and global scopes.

publicSurface.description: Defines the possible values for credential source: 'keychain' when backend is available, 'missing' when it is not.
Core class for secure credential operations (get, set, delete) using a pluggable backend. Consumed by LLM infrastructure and CLI commands.
Factory function that creates a CredentialStore for project-scoped secrets, deriving a unique secret name from the project root directory path.
Factory function that creates a CredentialStore for user-global secrets, deriving a unique secret name from the base directory path.
